title: After some 150 students walked out of Redlands schools early this month in support of immigrants, they were dealt an une
raw_response:
{"rating": -2.5, "lean": "Lean Left", "explanation": "This is a news report that covers student walkouts protesting Trump administration immigration enforcement. While the article includes multiple perspectives, the author's own framing choices consistently tilt toward sympathy for the student protesters and skepticism toward the conservative school board's disciplinary response. The accumulated directional patterns support a Lean Left classification. Detailed Analysis: (1) The headline and opening frame the disciplinary action as an 'unexpected consequence' dealt to students, casting it as something imposed upon them rather than a predictable result of rule-breaking, which builds sympathy for the students. (2) The author characterizes the conservative school board's response as standing 'in sharp contrast to the positive reception that student activism has received' elsewhere — the word 'positive' is the author's own editorial characterization, implicitly suggesting the supportive response is the better one. (3) The article devotes substantial space to student voices and sympathetic adult supporters (parent saying she's 'happy to see all these kids'), while the district's perspective is largely limited to a spokesperson's statements and the board's denial of ideological motivation. (4) The author provides detailed contextual background on the conservative board's other policies — book challenges and rainbow pride flag bans — framing them as ideologically aligned with the Trump administration, which implicitly links the disciplinary action to a broader conservative agenda despite the district's denial. (5) The ACLU's legal guidance and California civic absence law are presented at length, framing the legal landscape in ways that cast doubt on the legitimacy of the school's penalties. (6) The embedded article links ('We want a change to happen,' 'National shutdown,' 'Anti-ICE') all reference protest-sympathetic coverage, reinforcing a pro-protest editorial ecosystem. (7) The description of the MAGA-hat-wearing student being pelted with water bottles is reported matter-of-factly without the same sympathetic framing given to the protesting students, creating an asymmetry in how different participants are characterized.", "lean": "Lean Left"}
